eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Zach Werenski for the James Norris Memorial Trophy at 89.2% implied probability, driven by his elite regular-season dominance as Columbus Blue Jackets' top defenseman—22 goals to lead all blueliners, 81 points in 75 games (1.08 points-per-game pace despite missing time to illness), and top even-strength production that powered a rebuilding roster. Building on his runner-up finish last season, late-season NHL.com trackers cemented him ahead of Cale Makar, reflecting all-around impact with career-high ice time. Evan Bouchard's 95 total points and Edmonton Oilers' playoff surge represent the primary challenge, potentially swaying PHWA voters toward raw offense if power-play reliance is overlooked, though historical precedent favors consistent leaders like Werenski.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Ankara be 15°C on May 6?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 6 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Esenboğa Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tr/%C3%A7ubuk/LTAC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Official observations from Esenboğa International Airport (LTAC) station, the market's resolution source via Weather Underground, confirm Ankara's highest temperature reached exactly 15°C during midday hours on May 6, under persistent cloud cover and cool northerly airflow remnants from an early-month Arctic airmass. Turkish State Meteorological Service reports align, noting 13.8–14°C around noon with no further warming, consistent with GFS and ECMWF model consensus for subdued highs well below May's climatological average of 20–22°C. Trader consensus at 100% reflects this verified data, backed by METAR readings showing rapid evening cooldown to 7°C. Realistic challenges include a rare late-afternoon spike from clearing skies or post hoc data revision, though improbable given current trends and solar decline.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest KNMI and ECMWF forecast ensembles imply Amsterdam's highest temperature on May 7 clustering around 15-16°C, reflecting trader consensus amid high short-term uncertainty from variable cloud cover and possible light drizzle. BBC Weather guidance shows sunny intervals with a 15°C high, while drier GFS runs favor 16-17°C and cloudier scenarios hold 14°C viable, differentiated by the timing of showers trailing a recent frontal system. Following severe thunderstorms and hail in southern Netherlands on May 3 after a mid-week warm peak of 23-26°C southward, cooler Atlantic air dominates, with sea breeze effects potentially modulating coastal highs. New 00Z/12Z model updates on May 6 will clarify trajectories before resolution via official Schiphol observations.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures have retreated to around $95 per barrel after spiking above $102 amid escalating Middle East tensions, including Strait of Hormuz risks, prompting profit-taking following OPEC+'s May 3 announcement of a modest 188,000 barrels-per-day production increase for June. Polymarket trader consensus reflects this volatility, pricing 55% implied probability of hitting $115 and 52% for $110 by June-end settlement, with negligible odds below $90 amid high global inventories exceeding 8 billion barrels. A recent EIA report showed a bullish 6.2 million-barrel draw, but weak China demand growth caps upside; watch weekly EIA releases, June 7 OPEC+ review, and U.S. summer driving season for catalysts.

markets.question: Will Manchester City FC vs. Brentford FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 9, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 9°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Environment Canada's latest forecast, issued May 5 afternoon, projects a high of 11°C in Toronto on May 8 under cloudy skies with 60% chance of showers, reflecting a persistent cool upper-level trough steering moist air from the north and suppressing temperatures after recent observed highs near 9°C on May 6. However, trader consensus clusters tightly around 13–16°C at 20–22% implied probabilities each, driven by ensemble model spread—GFS and ECMWF analogs suggesting potential ridging or cloud breaks could boost highs by 3–5°C via increased insolation and boundary-layer mixing. Key differentiators include shower timing and coverage; full overcast favors lower outcomes like 11–13°C, while partial clearing tips toward 14–16°C. Watch for May 6 evening model updates from NOAA and EC, as 48-hour uncertainty remains high in spring's variable jet stream patterns.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán as the next leader out of power before 2027, driven by his Fidesz party's crushing defeat in the April 12 parliamentary election to Péter Magyar's Tisza party, which secured a supermajority of 141 seats amid voter backlash over economic stagnation, corruption scandals, and EU tensions. Orbán conceded on election night, relinquished his parliamentary seat on April 25, and offered to resign as Fidesz leader, while serving as caretaker PM until Magyar's scheduled swearing-in around May 9 following the new parliament's inaugural session by May 12. This commanding position reflects certainty of his imminent exit in Hungary's parliamentary system; realistic challenges would require unprecedented delays in government formation or presidential refusal to appoint the majority leader, both highly improbable given Tisza's dominance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Min Hyung-bae at 97.7% implied probability to win the June 3, 2026, inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayoral election, reflecting the Honam region's longstanding status as a Democratic Party stronghold where opposition People Power Party candidates historically poll below 20-30% amid high progressive turnout. Min, a sitting National Assembly member from Gwangju, unified the Democratic nomination in mid-April after a competitive runoff primary victory over Jeollanam-do Governor Kim Young-rok, consolidating party support without internal fractures. PPP's Lee Jung-hyun, former party chair with regional ties, recently proposed debates on integration and economic issues but faces structural barriers in this deep-blue battleground. Upsets would require a major Min scandal, national political shifts, or anomalous voter mobilization before election day.
